title: A Basket Of Flowers - From Dawn To Dusk
content:
آ آ آ آ Dawn

آ آ آ آ On skies still and starlit
آ آ آ آ White lustres take hold,
آ آ آ آ And grey flushes scarlet,
آ آ آ آ And red flashes gold.
آ آ آ آ And sun-glories cover
آ آ آ آ The rose shed above her,
آ آ آ آ Like lover and lover
آ آ آ آ They flame and unfold.

آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -

آ آ آ آ Still bloom in the garden
آ آ آ آ Green grass-plot, fresh lawn,
آ آ آ آ Though pasture lands harden
آ آ آ آ And drought fissures yawn.
آ آ آ آ While leaves not a few fall,
آ آ آ آ Let rose leaves for you fall,
آ آ آ آ Leaves pearl-strung with dew-fall,
آ آ آ آ And gold shot with dawn.

آ آ آ آ Does the grass-plot remember
آ آ آ آ The fall of your feet
آ آ آ آ In autumnï؟½s red ember,
آ آ آ آ When drought leagues with heat,
آ آ آ آ When the last of the roses
آ آ آ آ Despairingly closes
آ آ آ آ In the lull that reposes
آ آ آ آ Ere storm winds wax fleet?

آ آ آ آ Loveï؟½s melodies languish
آ آ آ آ In ï؟½Chastelardï؟½sï؟½ strain,
آ آ آ آ And ï؟½Abelardï؟½sï؟½ anguish
آ آ آ آ Is loveï؟½s pleasant pain!
آ آ آ آ And ï؟½Sapphoï؟½ rehearses
آ آ آ آ Loveï؟½s blessings and curses
آ آ آ آ In passionate verses
آ آ آ آ Again and again.

آ آ آ آ And I! I have heard of
آ آ آ آ All these long ago,
آ آ آ آ Yet never one word of
آ آ آ آ Their song-lore I know;
آ آ آ آ Not under my finger
آ آ آ آ In songs of the singer
آ آ آ آ Loveï؟½s litanies linger,
آ آ آ آ Loveï؟½s rhapsodies flow.

آ آ آ آ Fresh flowers in a basket,
آ آ آ آ An offering to you,
آ آ آ آ Though you did not ask it,
آ آ آ آ Unbidden I strew;
آ آ آ آ With heat and drought striving,
آ آ آ آ Some blossoms still living
آ آ آ آ May render thanksgiving
آ آ آ آ For dawn and for dew.

آ آ آ آ The garlands I gather,
آ آ آ آ The rhymes I string fast,
آ آ آ آ Are hurriedly rather
آ آ آ آ Than heedlessly cast.
آ آ آ آ Yon treeï؟½s shady awning
آ آ آ آ Is shortï؟½ning, and warning
آ آ آ آ Far spent is the morning,
آ آ آ آ And I must ride fast.

آ آ آ آ Songs empty, yet airy,
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve striven to write,
آ آ آ آ For failure, dear Mary!
آ آ آ آ Forgive me, Good-night!
آ آ آ آ Songs and flowers may beset you,
آ آ آ آ I can only regret you,
آ آ آ آ While the soil where I met you
آ آ آ آ Recedes from my sight.

آ آ آ آ For the sake of past hours,
آ آ آ آ For the love of old times,
آ آ آ آ Take ï؟½A Basket of Flowersï؟½,
آ آ آ آ And a bundle of rhymes;
آ آ آ آ Though all the bloom perish
آ آ آ آ Eï؟½en YOUR hand can cherish,
آ آ آ آ While churlish and bearish
آ آ آ آ The verse-jingle chimes.

آ آ آ آ And Eastward by Norï؟½ward
آ آ آ آ Looms sadly my track,
آ آ آ آ And I must ride forward,
آ آ آ آ And still I look back,
آ آ آ آ Look back, ah, how vainly!
آ آ آ آ For while I see plainly,
آ آ آ آ My hands on the reins lie
آ آ آ آ Uncertain and slack.

آ آ آ آ The warm wind breathes strong breath,
آ آ آ آ The dust dims mine eye,
آ آ آ آ And I draw one long breath,
آ آ آ آ And stifle one sigh.
آ آ آ آ Green slopes, softly shaded,
آ آ آ آ Have flitted and faded,
آ آ آ آ My dreams flit as they did,
آ آ آ آ Good-night! and, Good-bye!

آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -آ آ آ آ -

آ آ آ آ Dusk

آ آ آ آ Lost rose! end my story!
آ آ آ آ Dead core and dry husk,
آ آ آ آ Departed thy glory
آ آ آ آ And tainted thy musk.
آ آ آ آ Night spreads her dark limbs on
آ آ آ آ The face of the dim sun,
آ آ آ آ So flame fades to crimson
آ آ آ آ And crimson to dusk.
meaning: About: Adam Lindsay Gordon was an Australian poet, jockey and politician.
